sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: RELX Plc Hits Day Low of GBP 24.60 Amid Price Pressure
feed.summary: RELX Plc's stock significantly declined today, dropping by 5.24% to an intraday low of GBP 24.60, underperforming the broader FTSE 100 index. Despite this downturn, the company shows strong management efficiency with a high Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) and robust operating cash flow. However, it faces challenges including a substantial debt burden and elevated stock valuation metrics.

feed.title: New DR REDDYS (NYSE: RDY) director reports insider status with Form 3 filing
feed.summary: DR REDDYS LABORATORIES LTD director Arun Madhavan Kumar has filed an initial Form 3, establishing his status as a reporting insider. This filing, dated March 27, 2026, is a mandatory statement of beneficial ownership and does not indicate any transactions or holdings. It serves to set a baseline for future ownership disclosures by Mr. Kumar.

feed.title: [ARS] NORFOLK SOUTHERN CORP SEC Filing
feed.summary: This article announces an ARS SEC filing by Norfolk Southern Corp (NSC) on March 27, 2026, accessible as a PDF document. The filing has a low impact and neutral sentiment. The article also provides a company overview, financial data, recent news, and SEC filings for Norfolk Southern.

feed.title: Argan (NYSE:AGX) Raised to "Overweight" at JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. upgraded Argan (NYSE:AGX) from a "neutral" to an "overweight" rating, setting a new price target of $550.00, suggesting a potential upside of over 33%. This upgrade follows Argan's strong Q4 earnings, where the company reported EPS of $3.47 against an expected $1.99 and revenue of $262.1 million, driven by robust profitability with a net margin of 13.1% and ROE of 31.4%. Despite a high P/E ratio and recent insider selling, analyst consensus remains a "Hold" with an average target price of $391.20.

feed.title: Wells Fargo & Company Issues Pessimistic Forecast for Gartner (NYSE:IT) Stock Price
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for Gartner (NYSE:IT) from $150 to $140, assigning an "underweight" rating and suggesting a 10.56% downside. This pessimistic forecast follows similar target trims from other brokers and comes amidst a wave of securities-class-action filings against Gartner regarding alleged misstatements about consulting outlook and contract disclosures. Despite a consensus "Hold" rating among analysts with a higher price target, the legal issues and recent stock performance indicate a challenging period for the company.

feed.title: Emerson Electric (NYSE:EMR) Now Covered by Analysts at BMO Capital Markets
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets initiated coverage on Emerson Electric (NYSE:EMR) with a "market perform" rating and a $150 price target, suggesting a 19% upside. While analyst opinions are divided, with a consensus "Hold" rating, the company recently reported strong quarterly EPS of $1.46, surpassing estimates, and provided positive FY2026 guidance. Insider selling has occurred, but institutional investors hold a significant 74.3% stake in the company.

feed.title: Dover (NYSE:DOV) Earns Market Perform Rating from Analysts at BMO Capital Markets
feed.summary: BMO Capital Markets initiated coverage on Dover (NYSE:DOV) with a "market perform" rating and a $237 price target, suggesting a 13.2% upside. This comes as Dover recently beat quarterly earnings and revenue estimates, reporting $2.51 EPS and $2.10 billion in revenue. Despite insider selling, institutional investors have significantly increased their positions, with institutions now owning approximately 84.46% of the company's stock.

feed.title: Teleflex (NYSE:TFX) Upgraded by Raymond James Financial to "Outperform" Rating
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has upgraded Teleflex (NYSE:TFX) from a "market perform" to an "outperform" rating, setting a price target of $128.00, implying a 15.86% upside. This upgrade comes despite mixed analyst sentiment and Teleflex's recent significant earnings and revenue miss, though the company has provided 2026 guidance. Institutional investors have also shown varying activities in their holdings of the medical technology company.

feed.title: Top 3 Real Estate Stocks That Could Blast Off In March
feed.summary: This article identifies three real estate stocks—American Healthcare REIT Inc (AHR), Site Centers Corp (SITC), and Realty Income Corp (O)—that are currently oversold with an RSI near or below 30, suggesting a potential rebound. It highlights recent analyst ratings, stock performance, and company news for each, indicating they could "blast off" in March.

feed.title: Quantum Computing Inc. (NASDAQ:QUBT) Given Average Rating of "Hold" by Brokerages
feed.summary: Quantum Computing Inc. (NASDAQ:QUBT) has received an average "Hold" rating from six brokerages, with an average 1-year price target of $17.00. Recent analyst actions include both "sell" and "buy" ratings. The article also details recent insider stock sales by the CFO and COO, and institutional investor activity in QUBT shares.

feed.title: Settling into Sherwin-Williams’ HQ
feed.summary: Employees are moving into Sherwin-Williams' new headquarters in Cleveland, and while the building boasts state-of-the-art technology, there are still some initial issues being worked out, particularly with the interior systems. Despite these internal adjustments, plans for the building's distinctive exterior lighting package are still on track for implementation by the end of the year. Additionally, the first retail tenant for the parking garage's ground-floor space has been revealed as Century Federal Credit Union, a move facilitated by its existing relationship with Sherwin-Williams.

feed.title: P&G to Webcast Discussion of Third Quarter 2025/26 Earnings Results on April 24
feed.summary: The Procter & Gamble Company (NYSE:PG) announced it will webcast a discussion of its third-quarter earnings results on April 24, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. The live audio webcast will be accessible to media and investors at www.pginvestor.com, with a replay also available. P&G highlights its global presence and portfolio of major brands.

feed.title: Youth golf nonprofit gains Bank of America backing to reach more kids
feed.summary: Watson Links, a nonprofit youth golf mentoring organization, has partnered with Bank of America to expand its "Golf with Us" program. Bank of America will serve as Watson Links' Champion Partner, providing crucial funding to broaden mentor-led golf experiences nationwide, aiming to make golf more accessible and help young people develop life skills. This partnership is expected to increase access for thousands more junior golfers, building on the 1,153 participants in 21 U.S. markets in 2025 and the nearly 100,000 young people reached by "Golf with Us" last year.

feed.title: Medtronic Receives FDA Clearance for Stealth AXiS™ Surgical System for Cranial and ENT Procedures
feed.summary: Medtronic has received FDA clearance for its Stealth AXiS™ surgical system for cranial and ENT procedures, following its previous clearance for spine procedures. The system integrates surgical planning, navigation, and robotics into a single platform, utilizing AI-enabled architecture for advanced planning and visualization. This clearance highlights Medtronic's commitment to delivering connected technologies and supporting surgeons with enhanced precision and real-time insights across various specialties.

feed.title: Larry Fink and Making BlackRock Great Again
feed.summary: In this article, John Authers discusses Larry Fink's influential role as CEO of BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager. Authers highlights Fink's controversial annual letters to CEOs, where he advocates for his vision of capitalism, comparing them to Warren Buffett's letters but noting their greater tendency to generate debate. The article suggests that Fink's focus on "national capitalism" might be missing key drivers of US investment growth.

feed.title: A Meta data center deal aims to cut Louisiana customer costs by $2.65B
feed.summary: Entergy Louisiana announced an additional agreement with Meta, which is expected to deliver approximately $2 billion in customer savings over 20 years, bringing the total benefits to $2.65 billion when combined with a prior commitment. The deal involves significant infrastructure buildout, including new generation, transmission, and storage, primarily funded by Meta. This initiative supports renewable energy, enhances grid reliability, and includes specific funding for customer programs, positioning Louisiana as a high-tech and energy innovation leader.

feed.title: All lawsuits end as Credo and TE Connectivity strike AEC deal
feed.summary: Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd (NASDAQ: CRDO) and TE Connectivity Corporation (NYSE: TEL) have reached a confidential license and settlement agreement concerning Active Electrical Cable (AEC) technology. This agreement resolves all existing lawsuits between the two companies, which will now be dismissed. The terms of the settlement were not disclosed.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark Corp Stock: Stable Consumer Staples Leader with Defensive Appeal for North American I
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp (ISIN: US4943681035) is highlighted as a stable consumer staples leader, offering reliable dividends and resilient demand for North American investors. The article details its business strengths, including essential brands like Huggies and Kleenex, and its defensive appeal in economic uncertainties. It also covers financial health, strategic initiatives, and potential risks such as raw material volatility and competition.

feed.title: United Parks & Resorts (SeaWorld) Stock: Navigating Legal Challenges and Analyst Optimism Amid Theme Park Sector Pressures
feed.summary: United Parks & Resorts (SeaWorld) is facing a civil lawsuit from the U.S. Department of Justice concerning its wheeled walker policy, which is alleged to violate the Americans with Disabilities Act. Despite this legal challenge and recent earnings misses, Truist Financial has raised its price target for PRKS shares to $53, maintaining a buy rating and signaling confidence in the company's long-term recovery. The article discusses SeaWorld's business model, strategic positioning in the theme park sector, financial performance, and key risks and opportunities for North American investors.

feed.title: Humana Navigates a 47% Drawdown: Why the “Lifetime Value” Strategy Could Unlock a 312% Rebound
feed.summary: Humana (HUM) has experienced a significant 47% drawdown due to challenges in the Medicare Advantage (MA) sector, including high medical costs and flat government funding. However, the company is implementing a strategic shift focused on member "lifetime value" by improving retention and acquiring high-quality enrollees through better sales channels. This, combined with an expansion of its CenterWell primary care footprint, leads TIKR's model to project a 312% rebound to a target price of $718 if Humana successfully restores MA margins to 3% by 2028.

feed.title: Barclays Adjusts Aptiv Price Target to $77 From $105, Maintains Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Barclays has adjusted its price target for Aptiv (NYSE: APTV) shares, lowering it to $77 from the previous $105. Despite this reduction, the firm has decided to maintain its Overweight rating on the stock. This update reflects Barclays' continued positive outlook on Aptiv, albeit with a revised valuation.

feed.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley receives career‑high $27.5M compensation in 2025
feed.summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley received a career-high compensation package of $27.5 million in 2025, a significant increase from the previous year. This payout follows a profitable year for Ford and meeting most key targets, despite some industry challenges. The filing also detailed compensation for other top Ford executives, with a substantial portion tied to stock awards and future company performance.

feed.title: Camden Property Trust (CPT) installs new CEO, COO and CFO with updated contracts
feed.summary: Camden Property Trust (CPT) has announced a significant leadership restructuring effective March 24, 2026, promoting Alexander J. Jessett to CEO, Laurie A. Baker to President and COO, and Benjamin D. Fraker to EVP-CFO, while co-founder Richard J. Campo transitions to Executive Chairman. New employment agreements include enhanced severance benefits, offering up to 2.99 times Gross Income if termination without cause or resignation for good reason occurs around a Change in Control, and full vesting of service-based equity awards. These changes are part of a long-term succession plan aimed at ensuring strong future leadership, as detailed in an 8-K filing.

feed.title: Camden Property Trust Announces Promotions and Changes to Senior Leadership Team
feed.summary: Camden Property Trust (NYSE:CPT) has announced significant changes to its senior leadership team, including the promotion of Alexander J. Jessett to Chief Executive Officer and Laurie A. Baker to President and Chief Operating Officer. Benjamin D. Fraker has been promoted to Executive Vice President – Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer. These strategic leadership adjustments are part of Camden’s long-term succession plan, aiming to ensure continued strong and effective leadership for the company.

feed.title: New Nuclear ETF Swaps TSM for Broadcom in Rebalance
feed.summary: The ALPS Nautilus SMR, Nuclear & Technology ETF (SMRF) recently rebalanced its holdings, replacing Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) with Broadcom Inc. (AVGO). This adjustment reflects the fund's "compute + firm power" strategy, integrating AI infrastructure with nuclear energy providers. The rebalance involved 71 individual ticker changes, increasing exposure to uranium miners and small modular reactor developers, while trimming positions in broader utility services.

feed.title: Analog Devices Is Up 15% in 2026 and Wall Street Still Sees Room to Run
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) reported strong fiscal Q1 revenue of $3.16 billion, up 30% year-over-year, and issued optimistic Q2 guidance driven by record orders in its Data Center segment and a broad Industrial recovery. Analysts expect ADI's EPS to surge by 46.7% in fiscal 2026, with an estimated free cash flow (FCF) growth of 25.1%, funding its 22nd consecutive dividend increase. Wall Street analysts have a mean target price of $384.53, implying a 22.7% upside, with conviction anchored in AI infrastructure CapEx and the Industrial cyclical recovery, though the Automotive segment remains a variable.

feed.title: Applied Materials Overcomes a 21% Drawdown: How $700 Billion in AI CapEx Fuels the $431 Target
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has rebounded significantly after a 21% drawdown in late 2025, driven by an unprecedented surge in AI-related capital expenditures expected to reach $700 billion by 2027. The company is uniquely positioned to benefit from the demand for High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM) and has strong customer visibility with 2-year equipment orders. Despite trading at a discount to peers, TIKR's model projects a solid path to a $431 target price based on strong revenue growth and margin protection.

feed.title: Rocket Pharmaceuticals, Argan jump premarket; Two Harbors inches down
feed.summary: U.S. stock futures indicated a lower opening on Friday, while oil prices rose above $110 a barrel despite President Donald Trump's extension of a deadline for Iranian energy infrastructure attacks. Several companies experienced significant premarket動き: Rocket Pharmaceuticals and Lantern Pharma surged due to FDA approvals for their respective therapies, and Argan jumped on strong quarterly revenue. Conversely, Two Harbors Investment declined following its acquisition agreement.

feed.title: The Tesla Robotaxi Story Is A Myth: Why I'm Keeping My Strong Sell Into Q1 Earnings (TSLA)
feed.summary: The author maintains a Strong Sell rating on Tesla (TSLA), arguing that its robotaxi ambitions are a myth due to unproven autonomous capabilities, inferior data quality, and a high crash rate. The article highlights concerns about Tesla's shrinking core business, compressed margins, and an overreliance on sentiment rather than fundamental performance. With an expected decline in Q1 deliveries and R&D dependent on automotive cash flow, the author believes Tesla's business model faces significant risks.

feed.title: NXP Semiconductors Posts Strongest Forward Guide in Years: Wall Street Targets $261 Mean
feed.summary: NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) delivered a strong Q4 2025 performance, beating revenue and EPS estimates, and issued robust Q1 2026 guidance, signaling an end to its inventory correction. The company's growth is now driven by automotive accelerated growth drivers and a strong industrial segment, particularly in physical AI products. Wall Street analysts maintain a positive outlook with a mean price target of $261.32, anticipating significant upside as NXP's multi-year growth model re-engages.

feed.title: NYSE Owner Invests $600 Million in Polymarket, Capping Deal
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange Inc. (ICE), owner of the New York Stock Exchange, has completed a $600 million cash investment in Polymarket, a prediction markets platform. This investment finalizes ICE's plan to build a stake in Polymarket. Additionally, ICE anticipates purchasing up to $40 million in Polymarket securities from current holders.

feed.title: AQUESTIVE THERAPEUTICS, INC. (AQST) SHAREHOLDER ALERT
feed.summary: Bernstein Liebhard LLP reminds investors of Aquestive Therapeutics, Inc. (AQST) about an upcoming deadline in a securities fraud class action lawsuit. Investors who purchased shares between June 16, 2025, and January 8, 2026, and lost money, are encouraged to contact the firm by May 4, 2026, to discuss their legal options, including serving as lead plaintiff. The lawsuit alleges that Aquestive made misrepresentations regarding its New Drug Application for Anaphylm.

feed.title: Portland General Electric reduces staff by 53 workers to keep 'keep bills as low as possible'
feed.summary: Portland General Electric (PGE) has reduced its workforce by 53 positions, affecting 43 employees, as part of an effort to control costs and prevent future rate increases for customers. The utility stated these actions are a direct response to a challenging economic environment where rising operational costs are impacting customer bills. PGE aims to balance investment in grid modernization and clean energy with affordable rates for its customers.

feed.title: DR REDDYS (RDY) director submits Form 3 initial insider ownership
feed.summary: DR REDDYS LABORATORIES LTD director Kodumudi Pranatharthi Haran Krishnan has filed a Form 3, an initial statement of beneficial ownership. This filing formally establishes his insider status and records his initial holdings, setting a baseline for future disclosures without reporting any current buy or sell transactions. The Form 3 is important for investors as it ensures transparency regarding future changes in the director's beneficial ownership.

feed.title: DR Reddys (NYSE: RDY) director Penny Chan files initial Form 3
feed.summary: Penny Chan Wan, a director of DR REDDYS LABORATORIES LTD (NYSE: RDY), has filed an initial Form 3 with the SEC, reporting her beneficial ownership in the company's securities. This filing indicates no reported transactions or derivative positions at this time. The document confirms her role as a director, with no other beneficial ownership details or trading activities reported.

feed.title: VeriSign RSA 2026 Appearance Highlights Security Focus And Rich Valuation
feed.summary: VeriSign's Senior VP & CTO will present at RSA Conference 2026, an important event for the cybersecurity sector, highlighting the company's focus on security infrastructure and internet trust. This appearance offers insights into VeriSign's technical resource allocation and product priorities, though the stock is currently trading 83.2% above its estimated fair value. Investors should watch for commentary on encryption, DNS security, and future infrastructure investments, while also noting the company's high debt and negative shareholders' equity.

feed.title: Director Sanjay Mehrotra to exit CDW (CDW) board after 2026 meeting
feed.summary: CDW Corporation announced that director Sanjay Mehrotra will not seek re-election at the company's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Mehrotra will continue to serve through his current term, and his decision is not due to any disagreements with the company's operations, policies, or practices. This information was reported in an 8-K filing, indicating an orderly transition for the board.

feed.title: Is Pfizer’s Late‑Stage Lyme Vaccine Progress Reshaping the Investment Case for PFE?
feed.summary: Pfizer and Valneva's Phase 3 VALOR trial for their Lyme disease vaccine showed over 70% efficacy, marking significant progress as the furthest-advanced program in an area with no approved vaccines. This development, along with oncology trial successes, strengthens Pfizer's innovation narrative. However, the article notes that investors should also consider Pfizer's high dividend, rising debt, and free cash flow coverage as potential short-term risks despite the positive pipeline news.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise reshapes board with 5 newcomers and Elliott pact
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) announced a board refreshment, appointing five new independent directors and reaching a cooperation agreement with Elliott Investment Management L.P. John W. Chidsey was named Chairman, and four existing directors resigned, resulting in a nine-member board with eight independent directors. These changes aim to enhance shareholder value through improved governance and execution.

feed.title: There's A Lot To Like About Agilent Technologies' (NYSE:A) Upcoming US$0.255 Dividend
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies (NYSE:A) is set to pay a US$0.255 dividend, offering a trailing yield of 0.9%. The company demonstrates strong dividend sustainability with a low payout ratio of 22% of income and 28% of free cash flow, alongside robust earnings per share growth of 14% annually over the last five years. These factors, combined with a decade of consistent dividend growth, suggest an attractive investment for dividend-focused shareholders.

feed.title: Research Analysts Issue Forecasts for Campbell's Q3 Earnings
feed.summary: Zacks Research significantly reduced its Q3 2026 EPS estimate for Campbell's (NASDAQ:CPB) to $0.49 and downgraded the stock to a "Strong Sell," also lowering multi-year forecasts. This comes after Campbell's reported Q3 EPS of $0.51, missing the consensus, with revenue down 4.5% year-over-year. The company's shares are trading near their 52-week low but offer a high dividend yield of 7.4%, while analyst sentiment generally remains negative with an average target price of $27.65.

feed.title: Value Holdings Management CO. LLC Trims Position in Global Payments Inc. $GPN
feed.summary: Value Holdings Management CO. LLC reduced its stake in Global Payments Inc. (NYSE:GPN) by 24.4% in the fourth quarter, selling 69,500 shares and now holding 215,412 shares valued at approximately $16.67 million. Global Payments reported Q4 EPS of $3.18, slightly exceeding analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.25 per share. Analysts generally rate GPN as "Hold" with a consensus target price of $91.18.

feed.title: REX American Resources Corporation (NYSE: REX) Q4 2025 earnings call transcript
feed.summary: This is an earnings call transcript for REX American Resources Corporation's Q4 2025 financial results. It provides detailed discussions about the company's performance, financial figures, and operational insights for the quarter.

feed.title: [ARS] FEDERAL REALTY INVESTMENT TRUST SEC Filing
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) has filed an ARS form with the SEC. This filing, dated March 27, 2026, is categorized as having a low impact and neutral sentiment. The document is available as a PDF, and the trust has a market capitalization of $8.91 billion.

feed.title: Autodesk: Why Analysts Set a $332 Mean Target After Its $200 Million World Labs Bet
feed.summary: Autodesk (ADSK) recently reported strong Q4 results with billings up 33% and revenue exceeding analyst expectations, despite its stock being 21% below its 52-week high. The company's strategic $200 million investment in World Labs for AI development and the unification of its construction cloud platform into Forma are key drivers for future growth. Analysts have set a mean price target of $331.62, implying a significant upside, as they see Autodesk's commitment to AI-native design and a unified cloud ecosystem as undervalued by the current market.

feed.title: Labcorp Holdings Stock: Business Model, Market Position and Investor Considerations for US50540R4092
feed.summary: Labcorp Holdings Inc. (NYSE: LH) is a leading provider of clinical laboratory services, with its stock trading around $266 on the NYSE. The company's business model is divided into Diagnostics and Biopharma Services, benefiting from a vast network and strategic acquisitions in genomics and at-home testing. Labcorp's strong market position, solid financial health, and strategic growth initiatives position it as a defensive play for North American investors seeking exposure to the resilient healthcare sector, despite risks like reimbursement pressures and competition.

feed.title: Swedish defense orders more Teledyne underwater vehicles
feed.summary: Sweden's Defence Materiel Administration has placed a follow-on order for additional GAVIA autonomous underwater vehicles and payload modules from Teledyne Gavia, expanding on systems delivered in 2025. These vehicles will support Mine Countermeasures and Explosive Ordnance Disposal operations for the Swedish Armed Forces. Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY) has seen its shares rise by nearly 20% over the past year, though InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is currently overvalued.

feed.title: Quality, software revenues improved Ford's CEO 2025 compensation
feed.summary: Ford CEO Jim Farley's 2025 compensation package increased by 11% to $27.5 million, primarily due to a higher bonus linked to achieving quality and software revenue targets, despite the company's lower-than-expected adjusted operating profit. The compensation report also detailed packages for other executives, including Bill Ford and a new CFO, and noted Ford's net loss in 2025 due to EV-related charges.

feed.title: Record 2025 results drive Federal Realty (FRT) board, pay and ESG votes
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) is presenting its 2026 annual meeting proposals, including the election of eight trustees, an advisory "Say on Pay" vote for 2025 executive compensation, and the ratification of Grant Thornton as its auditor. The company highlighted strong 2025 financial performance with record revenue, increased NAREIT FFO per diluted share, robust leasing activity, significant acquisitions, and its 58th consecutive year of dividend increases. Additionally, Federal Realty reported substantial progress in ESG initiatives, including a 35% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions from 2019 through 2024.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy Stock Draws Fresh Focus on Dividend Yield, Nordea Stake and Houston Power Demand
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy's stock is under renewed scrutiny due to its dividend yield, Nordea Investment Management's increased stake, and surging power demand in Houston. The company projects a significant increase in Greater Houston's peak load by 2029, driven by data centers, and plans substantial capital spending to meet this demand. Despite market volatility and concerns about valuation and interest costs, investors are watching for stable earnings growth without burdening customers.
